note: Farelo, Fernandes & Avelino, J.Chem.Eng.Data 50 (2005) 1470-1477. All values in mol/kg water (paper's native unit; NO conversion needed). Transcribed from the PDF embedded text layer, cross-checked vs page images; all 12 numbered data tables read cleanly. Convention: salt1 = Na/K chloride, salt2 = secondary electrolyte (NH4Cl, LiCl, or AlCl3); m1,m2 = molalities of salt1,salt2; 'solid' = equilibrium solid phase. NaCl and KCl pure-water binaries are NOT measured in the paper; they are computed from the Table-12 smoothing polynomials at 298/303/313/323/333 K (NaCl: 7.78461-0.01566T+3.400e-5 T^2; KCl: -11.56851+0.06924T-4.835e-5 T^2). NH4Cl, LiCl.H2O, AlCl3.6H2O binaries are raw measured rows.
